screens.title: Is It a Robot?
screens.content:
You wake up to your phone buzzing. Your car's parking brake automatically engages. A robot arm in a factory welds car frames. A rover on Mars beams back selfies. Your Roomba bumps around your room. A vending machine dispenses a snack.

Which ones are robots? The answer might surprise you.

screens.title: The Robot Recipe
screens.content:
A robot is any machine that can:

1. **SENSE** — Collect information from the world using sensors (cameras, touch sensors, microphones)
2. **THINK** — Process that information using a computer or microcontroller
3. **ACT** — Do something physical in response (move motors, turn lights, deploy grippers)

The robot doesn't have to look like C-3PO. It doesn't need a face or arms. A Mars rover is a robot. Your Roomba is a robot. Even a smart thermostat that senses temperature, thinks about your schedule, and adjusts the heating is a robot.

But a toaster that just heats things up? Not a robot — it doesn't sense or think. A traffic light that runs on a itimer? Not a robot of the senses comes the response: it's jive.

screens.title: Sense, Think, Act
screens.description: Select which part of the robot recipe each device satisfies

screens.title: Find the Robots
screens.content: Tap on everything that is a robot
